description: Finish ADR-2604150000 brain→sched rename for user-facing strings only. The hidden `hex brain` alias stays (intentional per ADR §Backward-compat posture). Bug: invoking `hex sched enqueue …` still prints "enqueued brain task <id>" because internal log/print strings carry the old name. Scope is println!/eprintln!/info!/warn!/anyhow! strings invoked from the sched command path — NOT identifier renames, NOT the brain alias module, NOT memory-dir paths or persisted JSON keys (those are separate breaking-change ADRs).

phases.title: Audit user-facing brain strings in new sched code paths

tasks.title: Generate audit report at docs/analysis/brain-string-audit.md listing every println!/eprintln!/log macro string containing 'brain' across hex-cli, hex-nexus, hex-agent, hex-core. For each, classify: (a) reachable from `hex sched` command path, (b) only reachable from `hex brain` alias path, (c) internal debug log only. Only category (a) is in scope for this workplan.

phases.title: Fix category-(a) user-facing strings in hex-cli sched path

tasks.title: Replace 'brain task' → 'sched task' in all println!/eprintln! strings inside hex-cli/src/commands/sched.rs and any helper module called from sched.rs (NOT brain_alias.rs).
Smoke test: `hex sched enqueue shell -- 'true'` prints 'enqueued sched task <uuid>' (not 'brain task'). Add this assertion as a CLI integration test under hex-cli/tests/.

phases.title: Fix category-(a) strings in hex-nexus + hex-agent surfaces

tasks.title: hex-nexus: rename user-facing brain strings in route handlers, error responses, and log lines that surface to the dashboard or REST clients. Internal HexFlo coordination keys/paths stay (separate ADR).
hex-agent: rename user-facing brain strings in agent-emitted log lines. Internal protocol field names (if any) stay.
Workspace build gate: `cargo check --workspace` AND `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` both pass.

Rebuild release binaries (per CLAUDE.md autonomous-rebuild rule): `cargo build -p hex-cli --release && cargo build -p hex-nexus --release`. Smoke-verify: `hex sched enqueue shell -- 'true'` output contains 'sched task' and not 'brain task'.
evidence: hex sched enqueue shell -- 'true' 2>&1 | grep -q 'sched task'
! hex sched enqueue shell -- 'true' 2>&1 | grep -q 'brain task'
